Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505
Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:01am and concluding at 10:13am.

Plaintiff Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu appearing in court.
Defendant Demas Wai Yan not appearing.

Court examines Plaintiff Bryant Fu.

The matter is submitted.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter; - Matter not reported

Plaintiff Crystal Lei appeared with Bryant Fu in court.
No appearance by Defendant Demas Yan.

This matter is continued to January 21, 2026 at 9:00am in dept. 505. Mr. Fu is instructed to consolidate all the alleged debts including the request for rewal of judgment. He is instructed to file and serve a briefing on all this prior to the next calling of this matter.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Set For Trial On Dec-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Defendant Demas Wai Yan seeks judgment on the pleadings on plaintiffs Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu's complaint, styled as a "Verified Complaint for Action on Judgment Debts." Yan contends that all of the sanctions orders that provide the basis for Lei and Fu's complaint are already judgments. He argues that the Execution of Judgment Laws (EJL, codified at CCP 680.010 et seq.), provide the exclusive procedure for a party to renew a judgment. Yan argues that certain limits on the ability to renew a judgment-such as time requirements, and the fact that some judgments can be renewed only once-would be circumvented if Lei and Fu could roll all of the sanctions orders up into a new judgment.

"[N]othing in the EJL limits a judgment creditor's right to pursue an independent action on the judgment. (sec. 683.050.) An independent action " 'based on a judgment is an action based on contract. The [original] judgment becomes a debt which the judgment debtor is obligated to pay and the law implies a contract on his part to pay it. [Citation.]' [Citation.] Thus, when successful, an action on a judgment results in the entry of a new judgment.' " (Cadle Co. II, Inc. v. Fiscus (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1232, 1238 [citing Goldman v. Simpson (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 255, 262, fn. 3].) Indeed, even when a judgment cannot be renewed under the EJL, it may be enforceable in an action on the judgment. (See Green v. Zissis (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 1219, 1222.) On its face, Plaintiffs' complaint is properly pleaded. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Continued To Feb-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
